title: Finite Taylor Polynomial Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Taylor polynomial bad value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational Taylor replay for f(x)=1+2x+x^2 at center 1 and x=3/2 computes taylor_value = 25/4, while a malformed row claims taylor_value = 6, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Taylor rows only when they add distinct truncation, multivariable, analytic-convergence, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep Taylor theorem, remainder bounds, and convergence claims in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, center, degree, derivative values, factorials, Taylor coefficients, basis values, terms, Taylor value, polynomial value, and optional truncated remainder as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false Taylor value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through derivative, polynomial, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep Taylor theorem, remainder estimates, analytic convergence, radius-of-convergence, smoothness hypotheses, multivariable Taylor, floating-point Taylor evaluation, and general approximation theory marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
